snippet.title: ¿Se abrirá la puerta a TODAS las petroleras en Venezuela?
snippet.description:
¿Se abrirá la puerta a TODAS las petroleras en Venezuela?

El Departamento del Tesoro de Estados Unidos podría pasar de otorgar licencias individuales a empresas a emitir una licencia general que permita a cualquier compañía participar en la producción petrolera en Venezuela.

Esto cambiaría completamente el escenario de inversión: proyectos de miles de millones de dólares, reactivación de campos petroleros y nuevas reglas donde el Estado podría ajustar regalías según la rentabilidad y el tiempo de inversión.

La pregunta clave es: ¿estamos ante un nuevo ciclo de inversión petrolera en el país?

De hecho, en 2026 Washington ya ha emitido licencias generales que flexibilizan algunas restricciones y permiten operaciones vinculadas al petróleo venezolano bajo ciertas condiciones.

 ¿Crees que esta medida podría impulsar la producción petrolera de Venezuela?
Te leemos en los comentarios.

snippet.title: POTA Is Easier Than You Think
snippet.description:
Ready to take your amateur radio hobby into the great outdoors? Parks on the Air (POTA) is one of the fastest-growing activities in ham radio, and becoming an activator is easier than you think!

In this video, we break down exactly what you need to get out of the shack and into a park for your very first activation. We’re stripping away the complexity and focusing on the core essentials.

🎒 The "Must-Have" Gear List:

The Radio: Your gateway to the airwaves. Whether it’s a dedicated QRP rig or a portable 100W powerhouse, we’ll talk about what to look for.

The Battery: You can’t plug into a tree! We discuss LiFePO4 vs. traditional options for field use.

The Antenna: A decent antenna is the heart of your station. We look at EFHWs, verticals, and dipoles.

Antenna Tuner: How to ensure your match is perfect even when the trees aren't cooperating.

Coax Cable: The often-overlooked lifeline between your rig and the wire.

Logging Gear: Paper and pen or a digital app? We cover how to make sure those contacts count.

🌳 Why POTA?

POTA is about more than just making contacts; it's about radio portability, emergency preparedness, and enjoying our beautiful local and national parks. Whether you are a seasoned contester or a brand-new Tech, there is a place for you in the POTA community.

🔗 Helpful Resources:

Official POTA Website: https://parksontheair.com
POTA Spots (Real-time): https://pota.app

Don't forget to LIKE, SUBSCRIBE, and hit the BELL icon to follow my journey through the parks!

#HamRadio #POTA #ParksOnTheAir #AmateurRadio #PortableRadio #SOTA #FieldDay #QRP #POTAActivator

snippet.title: What Is a Copyleft License? GPL Explained Simply | Dev #Shorts
snippet.description:
Copyleft licenses, like the GNU General Public License (GPL), require that modified versions of open source software remain open source.

In this short video, we explain how copyleft works, what “share alike” means in practice, and why the GPL ensures that software improvements stay in the open source ecosystem. Unlike permissive licenses, copyleft licenses legally require derivative works to follow the same licensing terms.

If you’re working with open source software, understanding copyleft and GPL licensing is essential before you build or distribute your project.

snippet.title: Crystal Radio Extra Testing.    📻                                    #diyprojects #experiment #radio
snippet.description:
📻 Foxhole Radio Testing – No Battery!


This is a quick test of my homemade foxhole crystal radio. The radio works without any battery because the antenna captures energy from nearby AM broadcast signals. I used a razor blade and graphite pencil as a simple detector, a 400-turn air-core coil for tuning, and a homemade piezo crystal earphone.
To make the sound louder for recording, I connected my previously built LM386 audio amplifier, which is powered only by small solar panels.
This project demonstrates how radio signals themselves can provide enough energy to produce sound.



#FoxholeRadio #CrystalRadio #ElectronicsExperiment #HamRadio #DIYElectronics #SolarPowered

snippet.title: POTA Parks on the Air in Los Padres National Forest atop Cuesta Grade w/ some longer QSOs US-0639
snippet.description:
Hey, what's happening YouTube? 

I was up TV Tower Road in the Los Padres National Forest off Cuesta Grade off the 101. I made QSOs on 40, 20, 17, 15, 12, 10, 2 Meters and 70 Centimeters. Only 42 QSOs but lots of bands. When I refer to Pismo Dunes in the video, I realized after that those were the dunes at Montana De Oro State Park. LOL! I'm sure someone will correct me in the comments. See list below if our QSO made the video. I had some longer QSOs today. Nice to just chat once in a while. 73 de W6OBB

snippet.title: 염색방 창업은 이용사자격증이 유리한 이유 #shorts
snippet.description:

이용사 자격증 관련 내용의 영상입니다.

미용사 자격증은 한국산업인력공단에서 시행하는 국가기술자격증으로,
일반, 피부, 네일, 메이크업 총 4가지가 있고
필기와 실기 시험 둘 다 응시해서 합격해야 합니다.

이용사 역시 국가자격증이지만 남성 헤어만 다룬다는 점에서 다르죠.
필기는 미용이론, 공중보건학, 소독학, 피부학, 공중위생법규에 대한 내용으로
미용사보다 좀 더 과목 수가 적어 공부하기도 용이합니다.

휘게팔레트처럼 염색방 창업을 고민하고 계신다면
합격률이 좀 더 높은 이용사 자격증 취득을 추천드립니다.

오늘 영상은 여기까지 입니다.

snippet.title: The Contraption | Turn Your QMX Into An HF HT!
snippet.description:
The Contraption by @HamRadioDuo was something I saw a couple months ago and became slightly obsessed with.  It's a 3D printed design that houses your QMX, QDX, or QCX and incorporates an internal battery power bank and their Just OK Mini antenna as well as magnates on each side for mounting a CW key and cell phone.   The only problem is they made it for a slightly different battery bank.  That's were my obsession really went down the rabbit hole.  I had to learn how to modify the file in Bambu Studio to widen all 3 parts by 1mm to fit my battery.  Would you believe it actually worked, after many prototypes.  This is the story and build of this absolutely ridiculous Contraption.
⭐️⭐UPDATE:  Both versions of The Contraption are now up at https://hamradioduo.com/the-contraption-3d-files-available-for-the-preview-version/ for you to download whichever version of battery you have.  Scroll down for the K8MRD Redux Version if you have the same battery https://amzn.to/4ldxfhr as me.

snippet.title: Peak Operating: The SOTA Climbing Adventures of GW4BML
snippet.description:
Ben Lloyd GW4BML is a lifelong climber who discovered Summits on the Air (SOTA) and now combines both passions as he explores destinations across Wales and Scotland.

In this conversation on Q5 Worldwide Ham Radio, Ben traces his path from a childhood introduction to amateur radio at age twelve—thanks to a visit to Glyn GW0JAI’s shack—to eventually earning his full license decades later. For years, radio remained a background interest while climbing dominated his life. But when Summits on the Air entered the picture, everything clicked. Suddenly the peaks he loved became incredible operating spots, and the hobby transformed into something physical, portable, and deeply social.

That convergence led to a remarkable five-year stretch of family adventures built around summits, CW, and lightweight radio gear carried up steep trails. Ben shares the craft behind mountain operating—balancing antennas, batteries, and weather with the realities of high ground—and the unique satisfaction of making contacts from places where the station truly lives in your backpack.
Those experiences eventually became a book, Summit of Dreams, where Ben chronicles years of SOTA activations, climbing routes, and the people met along the way. The book captures both the technical side of operating portable radio in challenging environments and the human side of the hobby—how a simple radio on a mountaintop can connect strangers across continents and turn solitary climbs into shared adventures.

It’s a story about rediscovering radio through the landscape—and about how amateur radio can turn a solitary climb into a global conversation.

Join the conversation and subscribe to Q5 Worldwide Ham Radio.

From the shack to the summit, Icom keeps hams connected. We’re proud to have their support for Q5 Worldwide Ham Radio.

snippet.title: Astronauta por la borda?
snippet.description:
INFO EXTRA⬇️
El traje que ves en el video formaba parte del experimento SuitSat-1, realizado el 3 de febrero de 2006 desde la Estación Espacial Internacional. Los astronautas Valeri Tokarev y Bill McArthur liberaron un traje ruso Orlan ya retirado durante un paseo espacial y lo convirtieron en un pequeño satélite educativo. En su interior llevaba baterías, sensores y un transmisor de radio que emitía señales en 145.990 MHz, una frecuencia accesible para radioaficionados de todo el mundo.

snippet.title: Parks on the Air Morse Code contact with N7WPO, WA #HamRadio
snippet.description:
TN to WA QRP POTA contact with N7WPO.  

TU = Thank You
UR = You are or your 
5NN = 599 - RST signal report 
TN = Tennessee
BK = Break
GA = Good Afternoon 
WA = Washington 
RR = Roger Roger or received 
de = This is 
72 = Best regards (QRP handshake)
dit dit = cya

snippet.title: TUTORIAL UNTUK PEMULA || SETTING MANUAL HT KSUN UV60D
snippet.description:
TUTORIAL UNTUK PEMULA || SETTING MANUAL HT KSUN UV60D

📡 Deskripsi Lengkap HT KSUN UV60D
HT KSUN UV60D adalah handy talkie (HT) portabel multifungsi dengan dukungan dual-band VHF & UHF, mode frekuensi luas, dan fitur lengkap untuk komunikasi jarak jauh yang stabil. HT ini cocok untuk kegiatan outdoor, security, komunitas club, touring, camping, maupun penggunaan sehari-hari dengan jangkauan hingga ±5 km (tergantung kondisi medan). 
🔋 Fitur Utama
✔️ Dual-Band (VHF & UHF) – Dapat menerima dan mengirim pada pita frekuensi VHF (misalnya 136-174 MHz) dan UHF (misalnya 400-490 MHz) yang umum digunakan untuk HT. 
✔️ Multiband Receivers – Beberapa versi bisa menerima full band mulai dari frekuensi rendah sampai tinggi (contoh: 50-900 MHz) termasuk Airband dan band lain. 
✔️ Hingga 200 Channel – Penyimpanan frekuensi kanal banyak untuk mempermudah pengaturan dan pemanggilan cepat. 
✔️ Daya Output 5 Watt – Kekuatan sinyal tinggi untuk komunikasi lebih jauh. 
✔️ Layar LCD Besar – Tampilan jelas dengan informasi frekuensi, kanal, dan status baterai. 
✔️ Charger Type-C + Charging Base – Praktis dan modern, memudahkan isi ulang baterai tanpa kabel tambahan. 
✔️ Scrambler / Privacy Codes – Mendukung CTCSS/DCS dan DTMF untuk komunikasi lebih privat. 
✔️ NOAA Weather Alert – Fitur peringatan cuaca otomatis (tergantung versi). 
✔️ Reverse Frequency & Fast Channel Copy – Memudahkan pemrograman dan pemindahan 
🌐 Fungsi Penerimaan & Transmisi
🔹 RX (Receive):
• VHF/UHF umum
• FM Radio (76-108 MHz)
• Beberapa versi bisa menerima band tambahan seperti Airband 118-136 MHz. 
🔹 TX (Transmit):
• Dapat memancarkan sinyal pada frekuensi VHF/UHF yang diizinkan (tergantung versi & regulasi setempat). 
🛠 Spesifikasi Singkat
📶 Memori Kanal: ±200
⚡ Daya Output: ~5 W
🔋 Tegangan: ~7.4 V Li-ion
📺 Display: LCD
🔌 Charging: Type-C dengan base charger
📟 Mode Privasi: CTCSS/DCS, DTMF
🔔 Fungsi Tambahan: NOAA alert, scramble, reverse frequency. 
🌟 Kenapa HT Ini Layak Dipilih?
📌 Harga relatif terjangkau dibanding HT profesional lainnya. 
📌 Desain modern dengan charger Type-C. 
📌 Menyediakan banyak kanal dan fungsi suara privat untuk berbagai kebutuhan. 
📌 Bisa digunakan untuk hobi (ham radio/listen), kegiatan luar ruang, atau operasi lapangan. 
Terima kasih sudah menonton dan semoga video ini bermanfaat! 🙏

snippet.title: Нас готовят к страшному! В Москве нет мобильного интернета уже неделю. Мнения россиян. Опрос, Москва
snippet.description:
Мнение россиян о проблемах с полным отключением мобильного интернета в Москве уже неделю.

В столице начали масштабно обкатывать белый список, при этом всё же вернув горожанам мобильную связь. Местные власти уже почти неделю ничего не объясняют.

Сегодня в некоторых зонах в самом центре мобильный интернет продолжает не работать — сеть может откатываться до E.

Однако в некоторых районах ЦАО, которые чуть дальше от самого центра, мобильный интернет работает частично: открываются лишь некоторые сайты из белого списка.

Спуститься в метро и через Wi-Fi зайти в Telegram не получится: на некоторых ветках тоже ввели белый список.

snippet.title: Buying or selling a HF Rig? Watch Rob Sherwood First!! How-to knowledge.
snippet.description:
Thinking about buying a new HF radio? Before spending thousands on your next rig, listen to what receiver testing legend Rob Sherwood has learned from over 50 years of evaluating amateur radio equipment.

Rob is best known for the famous Sherwood receiver performance table, widely used by radio amateurs to compare the real-world performance of HF transceivers from manufacturers such as Yaesu, Icom, Kenwood, FlexRadio Systems, and Apache Labs.

In this long-form interview we explore what actually makes a great HF receiver, why dynamic range matters in crowded bands, and how operators can avoid the costly mistake of buying a radio that looks good on paper but struggles in real operating conditions.

Rob also explains common misunderstandings about receiver performance, discusses the evolution from traditional superheterodyne radios to modern SDR designs, and offers practical advice for choosing a radio that will perform well for DXing, contesting, and everyday operating.

If you enjoy deep discussions about receiver engineering, signal purity, dynamic range, and clean transmitters, this conversation is well worth your time.

Perfect for:

• Amateur radio operators considering a new HF transceiver
• DXers and contesters operating in crowded band conditions
• Radio enthusiasts interested in receiver design and performance testing
• Anyone who wants to avoid rig buying regret

⸻

⏱ Chapters

0:00 Introduction
1:50 Rob Sherwood introduction
2:08 Wrong assumptions – 50 years of testing knowledge
5:38 The dynamic range problem
6:58 Where are we today?
8:22 Podcasters’ myths and misconceptions
11:26 Why receiver performance is never just one number
13:51 OEM metrics and the top 26 transceivers
18:24 How to select a new radio
21:05 AGC thresholds explained
23:17 Blocking dynamic range
24:40 SSB sensitivity, noise and selectivity
33:51 Common mistakes and knowing your rig
37:55 Direct sampling radios
39:17 Dirty vs clean transmitters – cleaning up the bands
49:40 Important factors to consider when buying a radio
51:49 Audience Q&A with Rob – real buying context
1:16:00 Tribute song to Rob Sherwood

⸻

📻 Topics covered

HF receiver performance
Dynamic range and blocking
Direct sampling SDR radios
AGC behaviour and thresholds
SSB sensitivity and noise floor
Clean transmitters and IMD
Choosing the best amateur radio transceiver
